title: New Testament Foundations — Luke
subtitle: The Gospel of the Universal Savior
description: A structured study of Luke's Gospel tracing Jesus as Savior for all people — including the poor, women, Samaritans, and Gentiles — whose journey to Jerusalem fulfills God's redemptive purpose for the world.
learning_outcomes: Identify Luke's stated purpose and his intended audience
Explain the significance of Luke's travel narrative and its theological function
Describe Luke's distinctive concern for the marginalized and the outsider
Trace the role of the Holy Spirit throughout Luke's Gospel
Explain the connection between Luke and Acts as a two-volume work

theological_framework.emphasis: Salvation history and the universal scope of the gospel

units.title: The Journey to Jerusalem
units.description: Teaching on the road and approach to the city (Lessons 9–13)

instructional_approach.session_structure: 7-segment lesson flow (orientation, reading, context, analysis, themes, question, close)
instructional_approach.knowledge_assessment: factual recall questions at lesson end

questions: What does Luke state as his purpose in Luke 1:1-4?
What is the Magnificat and who speaks it?
Where does the long central section of Luke begin and end?
Name two parables unique to Luke that illustrate God's grace to the lost.
Where does Luke's Gospel end geographically?

next_course.title: New Testament Foundations — John
next_course.description: Study John's theological Gospel of signs and belief
